description: This figure displays electron backscatter diffraction (EBSD) Schmid factor distribution maps showing the evolution of austenite and B2 grains with increasing strain (0%, 1%, 3%, and 6%) in an Fe-30Mn-9Al-1C-1V-5Ni duplex lightweight steel. Panels (a1) to (d1) correspond to the austenite (γ) phase with a color scale ranging from 0 to 0.5, while panels (a2) to (d2) represent the B2 phase with a scale from 0 to 0.2; all maps include 5 μm scale bars. The color variations within individual grains indicate orientation-dependent slip system activation in each phase. With progressive straining, the Schmid factor distributions evolve distinctly between the two phases, reflecting heterogeneous deformation and strain partitioning across the soft austenite matrix and the harder B2 phase. These maps provide direct microstructural evidence for cooperative deformation mechanisms and support the analysis of geometrically necessary dislocation accumulation and hetero-deformation induced (HDI) strengthening in the duplex steel.
